mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy that lowers equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in unemployment rate above 8.9% indicates deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high UNRATE periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal fails to predict 60%+ of negative return instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and industrial production signals strong economic demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through cash flow optimism.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and INDPRO fails to predict positive 60d SPX returns in > 60% of cases or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening, creating headwinds for equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high CPIAUCSL periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's predictive power drops below 55% accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing capacity, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20d SPX returns in > 50% of cases or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in housing starts (HOUST) above 510k signals improved consumer confidence and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high HOUST periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and falling interest rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a supportive environment for equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS growth and FEDFUNDS decline fails to predict positive 60d SPX returns in > 50% of cases or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in retail sales (RSAFS) above 337,677 million dollars indicates strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during post-recession recoveries.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 20d SPX returns in > 55% of post-recession periods or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.03.
